description: Migration V6 reviewed coarse-to-fine alignment geometry and capture contract.

workflow_interface.python_motion_boundary: Python records and proposes. Guarded YASE performs operator-confirmed motion.

canonical_axes.legacy_alias_policy: x, y, and z are accepted only as normalized input aliases; schema-2 output uses machine_x_um, machine_y_um, and machine_z_um.

final_clearance_model_um.coordinate_frame: reviewed final rectangle-relative machine X/Y/Z frame

gross_top_xz.purpose: Bring the active ball safely into the expected frame.
gross_top_xz.reuse_rule: Only the matching gross view and reviewed standard baseline may be compared.

fine_top_xz.purpose: Measure ball X/Z relative to the laser rectangle center.
fine_top_xz.scale_source: reviewed 500 um laser rectangle short edge
fine_top_xz.reuse_rule: Reference and ball captures must have the same fine-top view, zoom, and image dimensions.

mirror_side_y.purpose: Measure ball Y relative to the physical trench top.
mirror_side_y.reuse_rule: The side scale is valid only for the reviewed mirror view and zoom.

capture_memory.replacement_rule: A re-recorded capture replaces its active record, archives the prior revision, and invalidates all downstream correction plans, convergence states, transitions, and final verification.
capture_memory.cancel_rule: Review cancellation does not update memory.
capture_memory.stability_rule: All camera, zoom, and tower axes are queried before and after the grab; excessive motion rejects the capture.
capture_memory.settings_rule: Standard exposure and three light outputs are reapplied before the operator focus/framing gate and stored as commanded values; the operator must not change them because analog readback is not claimed.

standard_position_order: raise every tower with an X/Z target to its reviewed clearance Y
move tower machine Z before machine X
lower each tower to final machine Y only after lateral motion
motion_policy.second_ball_correction_order: Evaluate both bounded machine X/Z axis orders in the final common frame and select the strict-clearance path with the largest projected ball-to-ball surface gap.
motion_policy.side_insertion_order: Validate strict common-frame insertion clearance before starting the top-to-side transition, then require that completed transition before any image-derived machine Y correction.

final_verification: all six correction captures converged at their active revisions

fail_closed_requirements: No correction without a current reviewed capture at the supplied exact machine pose.
No fine-top correction when view, zoom, or image dimensions differ.
No side Y correction for missing, duplicate, reversed, out-of-ROI, or implausibly separated mirror ruler features.
No correction outside the configured total residual or per-step bound.
No repeated correction from the same capture revision.
No continued convergence when the residual increases.
No lateral tower correction below the reviewed standard-position machine Y boundary.
No side insertion before the active top-view capture is converged and its top-to-side transition is complete.
No top-to-side lowering transition unless the reviewed top geometry preserves strict source, ball, taper, and trench-floor clearance.
No second-ball X/Z correction when neither bounded axis order preserves a strictly positive projected ball-to-ball surface gap.
No backward re-entry into ball 1 correction or transition motion while active ball 2 records remain in memory.
No final pass unless source, ball-to-ball, taper, and trench-floor surface gaps are strictly positive.
No YASE transition anchor without a current reviewed source capture and a matching converged revision when that source has an offset correction.
No direct hardware motion from Python.
commissioning_boundary: Repository tests and static audits validate ordering and reviewed common-frame geometry, not the raw stage-to-object collision envelope. Signs, soft limits, gripper and trench volumes, clearance Y coordinates, waits, and mirror behavior require guarded machine commissioning.
